name: Munenori Kawasaki
facts: Famous for his high-energy post-game interviews and 'I am Japanese!' catchphrase.
Authored a 'handbook' on how to be a teammate.
Once told reporters his secret to avoiding cramps was eating three bananas a day.

name: Ken Harvey
facts: The lone representative for the Royals in the 2004 All-Star Game.
The guy you would not want charging the mound at you before Jorge Soler became that guy.

name: Willie Bloomquist
facts: Played for and coached the Arizona State Sundevils baseball team.
The ultimate utility man. He played multiple positions throughout his career.
Kindof looks like Derek Lowe and Justin Verlander had a baby.

name: Jason Bay
teams: Padres, Pirates, Red Sox, Mets, Mariners
facts: Canadian
The first Pirate to win the National League Rookie of the Year award since Barry Bonds in 1986. The last Pirate to win the award prior to Paul Skenes.
About equally as attractive as Paul Skenes.

name: Esteban Loaiza
teams: Pirates, Rangers, Blue Jays, White Sox, Yankees, Nationals, A's, Dodgers
facts: Married to Mexican Singer Jenni Rivera until her death in 2012. Who? Ask any 40+ year old Mexican woman about Jenni Rivera.
Was arrested for possessing over 40 pounds (yes 40 pounds) of cocaine in 2018 and sentenced to 3 years in prison.
Upon his release from prison, he was deported in 2021 before being deported was cool

name: Mike Cameron
teams: White Sox, Reds, Mariners, Mets, Padres, Brewers, Red Sox, Marlins
facts: One of the key trailblazers in the tilted cap movement that was recently concluded by Fernando Rodney.
Played eight different teams in his 17-year career, but is most remembered for his time with the Seattle Mariners.
Notably took over for Ken Griffey Jr. in center field for the Mariners after Griffey was traded to the Reds.

name: Lyle Overbay
teams: Diamondbacks, Brewers, Blue Jays, Pirates, Yankees, Braves
facts: Although just a rookie bench warmer, he was a member of the 2001 World Series Champion Arizona Diamondbacks.
Finished his career with a 0.00 ERA after retiring Ryan Doumit, the only batter faced in his lone pitching appearance.
Known as Lyle Overpaid for his large contract with the Blue Jays.

name: Miguel Cairo
teams: Blue Jays, Cubs, Cardinals, Yankees, Mets, Mariners, Phillies, Reds
facts: As does any Venezuelan born in the 1970s, he has ten siblings.
The true personification of a 'that guy is still around?' player come every opening day during the 2000s.
Has the perfect baseball name.

name: Ronnie Belliard
teams: Brewers, Rockies, Indians, Cardinals, Nationals, Dodgers
facts: Nicknamed 'La Pared' (The Wall) for his defensive skillset.
Was a member of the 2006 World Series Champion Cardinals.
The Dodgers put a clause in his contract that he had to stay under 210 pounds.

name: Fernando Vina
facts: Made an appearance in the 'Welcome to Atlanta (Coast 2 Coast Remix)' music video by Jermaine Dupri featuring Nelly and the St. Lunatics
Posted an incredibly low 6.2% strikeout percentage over his career.
While his mom was pregnant with him, she attempted to flee Cuba twice on a boat. Eventually his parents were successful and arrived in Key West in 1968.
